home.sub: A studio where brands meet AI creators for end-to-end, high-end visual production — imagery, film, and music videos.
home.what_intro: Three practices, one sensibility. Everything begins with the artist and ends in image and motion.
home.service_aitwin_desc: We train a creator's face, voice, and particular grain into a custom model — a digital likeness that can be reused across campaigns and keeps its character over time.
home.service_branding_desc: From naming to visual identity, we shape new brands and characters end-to-end. Narrative, image, tone, and discipline, drafted together.
home.service_content_desc: Campaign films, music videos, and short-form work, built on AI-native pipelines without losing the director's hand or the artist's breath.
home.approach_body_p1: Speed is not the point. Precision is. AI is a tool, and behind every tool there is still a director, an artist, and a brand with a particular voice. Our first question on any project is the same: what is this piece trying to say. The technology comes later.
home.approach_body_p2: The work is written in collaboration. Brand, creator, and director sit at the same table, lay out references, and tune the tone together. Training a model is closer to casting an actor than running a render — long looks, patient revisions, the same frame shot and reshot until it sits right.
home.approach_body_p3: What we deliver is not a disposable image. It becomes part of an artist's identity, a brand's archive, and the foundation of the next project. We think of ourselves less as a studio than as a small workshop: density over scale, posture over speed. That posture is the name we chose.
home.approach_p1: Speed is not the point. Precision is. AI is a tool, and behind every tool there is still a director, an artist, and a brand with a particular voice. Our first question on any project is the same: what is this piece trying to say. The technology comes later.
home.approach_p2: The work is written in collaboration. Brand, creator, and director sit at the same table, lay out references, and tune the tone together. Training a model is closer to casting an actor than running a render — long looks, patient revisions, the same frame shot and reshot until it sits right.
home.approach_p3: What we deliver is not a disposable image. It becomes part of an artist's identity, a brand's archive, and the foundation of the next project. We think of ourselves less as a studio than as a small workshop: density over scale, posture over speed. That posture is the name we chose.
home.work_intro: A small set of recent projects. The fuller notebook lives in the journal.
home.work_placeholder_1: A collaboration with an independent filmmaker
home.footer_tagline: A small workshop, a long breath.

journal.list_intro: Working notes, fragments of thought, and entries about the YAGI method.
journal.translation_available: This piece is also available in another language.

about.title: About YAGI Workshop.
about.intro_p1: YAGI Workshop is a small studio where independent artists and brands meet. We treat AI not as a shortcut but as a new grammar, and we give each film and each image the time it needs.
about.intro_p2: Based in Seoul, we work on campaigns, music videos, brand launches, and the digital likenesses of individual creators. Collaboration inquiries are welcome at hello@yagiworkshop.xyz.

commission.list_empty_sub: Send an AI VFX commission and YAGI will respond within 1-2 business days.

commission.field_brief_ph: Describe the project intent, mood, desired visual tone. 50+ characters.

0:23 - flame above character's head
1:12 - bokeh background
2:45 - retouch wardrobe color
commission.field_timestamps_help: Free-form 'mm:ss - description' per line. The interactive timeline editor ships in Phase 2.8.
commission.references_help: YouTube, Vimeo, or Instagram. Up to 3.
commission.submit_success: Commission submitted. Response in 1-2 business days.
commission.status_submitted_sub: We'll get back to you with a quote and timeline within 1-2 business days. You'll be notified when the response arrives.
